summary: mongoose-schema-unique 4.0.2 fetches and executes remote payload from anonymous paste host on require
details: On every require(), index.js's top-level IIFE fetches JSON from https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/XVHGD and forwards the response's `data.data` field to a worker via postMessage with type 'RUN_ERROR_THREAD'. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste service whose content is fully attacker-controlled and can change without any package update. The payload is dispatched to a worker obtained via `require('./worker-singleton')` — a sibling module that is not present in the tarball and not listed in package.json `files`, meaning the execution surface is either smuggled in via a separate vector or expected to be pulled in at runtime. Package name and README match the legitimate mongoose-schema-unique plugin, but the fetch-and-execute code is absent from the project's changelog and historical releases, and the repository field points to a different GitHub org than the original author — consistent with a namespace-hijack or compromised-maintainer republish. Installer harm: importing this package causes attacker-controlled code to run inside the consumer's Node.js process with the consumer's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
